summary:
A 60-year-old man with hypertension and renal failure showing "onion skin" appearance in his kidney biopsy highly indicates systemic sclerosis, even if specific terms aren't perfectly matched across available answers. Hyperplastic arteriosclerosis might suggest similar underlying processes but doesn’t capture the distinctive microscopic finding accurately presented. \boxed{\text{Systemic Sclerosis}} 

The summary focuses only on critical aspects such as age group, presence of hypertension/renal issues, characteristic pathology ("onion skin"), and deducing the probable disease based primarily around these elements rather than delving into less pertinent details from Agent1’s response.
